macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Slows to 3.5% in June, Below Expectations
items.summary: U.S. inflation dropped to 3.5% in June, lower than the 3.8% analysts expected, driven by a break in gasoline prices and offering relief to consumers.

items.title: Trump Imposes 50% Tariffs on Canadian Goods
items.summary: The U.S. announced new 50% tariffs on a wide range of Canadian products including wine, dairy, and hockey sticks, citing discriminatory treatment of U.S. cars, alcohol, and dairy.

items.title: Asian Stocks Rise as Middle East Mediation Lowers Oil Prices
items.summary: Asian equities gained on Tuesday as mediation efforts between the U.S. and Iran pushed oil prices away from a one-month high, though tensions remain over energy supplies.

items.title: Oil Prices Dip Below $90 Amid Iran War Talks
items.summary: Crude oil softened on Tuesday after dipping from a one-month high near $90, as reports of U.S.-Iran mediation offset fresh attacks and threats of a naval blockade.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%, marking a shift in the AI-driven stock market leadership.

items.title: Morgan Stanley Posts Record Q2 Revenue as Equities Trading Surges 69%
items.summary: Morgan Stanley reported record quarterly revenue and profit, driven by a 69% surge in equities trading, with rivals JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs expected to follow.

items.title: Kevin Warsh Named New Federal Reserve Chair, Launches Policy Task Forces
items.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has formed five task forces to review monetary policy and faces the challenge of shrinking the Fed's $6.7 trillion balance sheet without destabilizing banks.

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Slows to 4.3%, Weakest Since 2022
items.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% in the second quarter, the slowest pace since 2022, despite strong export growth and a trade surplus exceeding $1.2 trillion.

items.title: S&P 500 Edges Down 0.19% as AI Stocks Face Pressure
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 0.19% over the past 24 hours as slumping AI stocks and chipmakers dragged down global markets amid earnings tests for the AI trade.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Reclaims $65,500 as US-Iran Talks Resume, Adding $44B to Crypto Market
items.summary: Bitcoin surged to a two-week high near $65,500 after reports suggested US-Iran talks may resume, injecting $44 billion into the crypto market in two hours, though it later dipped to hover around $64,000.

items.title: Grayscale Files for Spot Worldcoin (WLD) ETF with SEC
items.summary: Major asset manager Grayscale officially registered intentions to launch a spot ETF for Worldcoin's WLD token, prompting WLD's price to break higher as the filing moves toward SEC review.

items.title: Trump Accepts Ethics Provisions, Advancing CLARITY Act Tow Senate Vote
items.summary: President Trump agreed to ethics provisions limiting his family's financial activities, removing a key obstacle for the CLARITY Act crypto market structure bill to proceed to a Senate vote by early August.

items.title: Washington Judge Blocks Kalshi's Sports Prediction Markets Over State Gambling Laws
items.summary: A federal judge in Washington ruled that Kalshi's sports prediction markets violate state gambling laws, blocking the platform's operations in the state and causing open interest on Kalshi and Polymarket to fall 20%.

items.title: SEC Charges Florida Man and Company in $22M Crypto Mining Fraud Scheme
items.summary: The SEC filed charges against a Florida individual and his company for allegedly orchestrating a $22 million fraud involving crypto mining operations, marking a significant regulatory enforcement action.

items.title: BlackRock Acquires $116M in Bitcoin, Boosting Institutional Presence
items.summary: BlackRock purchased an additional $116 million in Bitcoin, reinforcing institutional confidence as US spot BTC ETFs recorded a $227 million net inflow, the highest daily figure since July 10.

items.title: HashKey Partners with Kbank for Korean Won Stablecoin in Cross-Border Payments
items.summary: HashKey Group signed an MoU with Kbank and BPMG to explore using a compliant Korean won stablecoin for cross-border payments, combining institutional support with blockchain infrastructure.

items.title: Metaplanet Subsidiary Secures $59.5M for Bitcoin Purchases
items.summary: Bitcoin Japan, a subsidiary of Metaplanet, secured $59.5 million via convertible bonds to fund additional Bitcoin acquisitions, joining the parent company's existing 43,000 BTC holdings.

items.title: Polymarket Faces Scrutiny Over $200M Suspected Insider Trading
items.summary: Analysts flagged nearly $200 million in suspected insider trading on Polymarket, prompting the platform to strengthen monitoring and report approximately 100 suspicious wallets to authorities.

items.title: ENS DAO Approves Security Council with Emergency Veto Powers
items.summary: ENS DAO approved an eight-member security council equipped with emergency veto powers to prevent governance attacks while preserving normal community decision-making processes.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's First Open 3T-Class Model, Then Pauses Subscriptions Due to Demand
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI launched Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model with a 1-million-token context window, claiming it is the first open frontier model in the 3T class; the company temporarily paused new subscriptions as demand overwhelmed capacity.

items.title: Nvidia Unveils Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B On-Device World Model for Physical AI and Robotics
items.summary: Nvidia announced Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4-billion-parameter Nemotron-based world model that runs on-device for vision reasoning and robot policy generation across Jetson, RTX, and DGX systems, with 22 Japanese robotics giants signing on to its ecosystem.

items.title: Google Developing 'Frozen v2' Server Chip Embedding Gemini Architecture in Silicon for 6–10x Efficiency Gain
items.summary: Google is reportedly building a new server chip called Frozen v2 that permanently etches parts of its Gemini AI model into silicon to skip decisions and reduce data movement, projected to launch by 2028 with 6 to 10 times higher efficiency than current TPUs.

items.title: Anthropic Settles Author Class Action for $1.5B Over Claude Book Misuse; Gives Teachers Free Premium Claude
items.summary: A federal judge approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement with authors who accused the company of misusing books to train Claude, while the company also announced it is providing every teacher free premium access to Claude.

items.title: Alibaba Previews Qwen3.8-Max-Preview and Announces Open-Weight Qwen3.8 with 2.4T Parameters
items.summary: Alibaba Group made Qwen3.8-Max-Preview available through its Token Plan and Qoder products and announced Qwen3.8, a 2.4-trillion-parameter model set to be released as open-weight soon, signaling China's rapid progress in rivaling U.S. AI systems.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Red, an Automated Red-Teaming Model for Prompt Injection Vulnerabilities
items.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming model trained via self-play reinforcement learning to detect and exploit prompt injection vulnerabilities in AI systems.

items.title: Sakana AI Releases Fugu-Cyber, Multi-Agent Model for Enterprise Cybersecurity
items.summary: Sakana AI introduced Fugu-Cyber, a multi-agent orchestration model purpose-built for enterprise cybersecurity, now available as a new API endpoint.

items.title: Trump Administration Considering Controls on Access to Frontier AI Models and Use of Chinese Open-Weight Models
items.summary: The Trump administration is taking steps to control access to the latest frontier AI models and is reportedly reconsidering ways to discourage U.S. use of Chinese open-weight models following Kimi K3's emergence.

items.title: Samsung Creates CEO-Level Robotics Division to Accelerate Commercialization
items.summary: Samsung Electronics announced it will establish a robotics division reporting directly to the CEO to accelerate development and commercialization, positioning robotics as a key growth engine.

items.title: New York Bans Data Center Construction for One Year, Rattling AI Industry
items.summary: New York imposed a one-year moratorium on data center construction, a move that could become a blueprint for anti-AI movements and is already impacting AI infrastructure planning.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Oblivion Remastered Confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2 on August 11
items.summary: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered will launch on Nintendo Switch 2 on August 11, running at 30fps in both docked and handheld modes.

items.title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Beta Early Access Starts August 21
items.summary: Activision announced the Call of Duty: NEXT showcase on August 21st, with beta early access for pre-orders beginning August 21 and a worldwide open beta from August 28 to September 1 on all platforms including Switch 2.

items.title: Bethesda and id Software Hit Hard by Microsoft Layoffs
items.summary: Reports indicate up to 50% of some teams at Bethesda and id Software were affected by recent Microsoft layoffs, with more reductions potentially coming.

items.title: Nintendo Ends Support for Mario Kart Tour in September
items.summary: Nintendo is retreating further from mobile gaming by ending support for Mario Kart Tour this September, though it recently launched a new mobile title called Pictonico.

items.title: Midgar Studio Ceases Operations
items.summary: Midgar Studio, known for Edge of Eternity and Edge of Memories, has officially closed operations as announced by founder Jérémy Zeler-Maury.

items.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4 Releases September 17 with New Necron Trailer
items.summary: Deep Silver and KING Art Games released a cinematic trailer highlighting the Necrons' return for Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4, which launches on PC on September 17, 2026.

items.title: Marathon's First Experimental PvE Mode 'Vault Breaker' Launches Today
items.summary: Marathon's new PvE mode Vault Breaker introduces roguelite progression and arrives today, while the game's director steps down with a replacement already named.

items.title: Dragon Ball: The Breakers Removed from Active Games List Ahead of Shutdown
items.summary: Bandai Namco has removed Dragon Ball: The Breakers from its active game list after four years of multiplayer, signaling the imminent closure of the game's online services.

items.title: Steam Train Fest Sale Runs Through July 27
items.summary: Steam launched its themed Train Fest sale offering discounts on train-related games for one week, ending July 27.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Price to Increase by $50 on September 1
items.summary: Due to changing market conditions, the Nintendo Switch 2 will see a $50 price bump starting September 1, 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Launches First U.S. Co-Branded Credit Card with Barclays
items.summary: Samsung Electronics launched its first U.S. co-branded credit card with Barclays, marking a significant expansion into financial services and signaling plans to broaden its offerings in the country.

items.title: TSMC Plans $100 Billion Investment to Expand U.S. Manufacturing Capacity
items.summary: Major Taiwan chipmaker TSMC announced plans to spend an additional $100 billion expanding its manufacturing capacity in the United States, reinforcing its commitment to U.S. semiconductor production.

items.title: Amazon's Adaptive Display Accessibility Feature Rolls Out for Fire TVs
items.summary: Amazon officially began rolling out its Adaptive Display accessibility feature for Fire TVs today, enhancing usability for users with visual impairments.

items.title: First UL 3700-Compliant Plug-in Solar Microinverter Available in U.S.
items.summary: The first plug-in solar microinverter compliant with UL 3700 safety standards is now available in the United States, advancing residential solar energy adoption.

items.title: FCC Plans to Ban Companies Selling DJI Products Under Other Brands
items.summary: The FCC is preparing to ban companies from selling DJI drone products under alternative brand names, aiming to prevent circumvention of existing restrictions on the Chinese manufacturer.

items.title: San Francisco Orders Apple and Google to Remove 'Nudify' Apps
items.summary: San Francisco has issued an order requiring tech giants Apple and Google to remove applications that generate sexually explicit deepfakes from their platforms.

items.title: IQE Raises Full-Year Revenue Forecast After First-Half Trading Beat
items.summary: UK semiconductor wafer maker IQE increased its full-year revenue growth forecast following a first-half trading performance that exceeded expectations due to rising demand for its products.
